label: LSTMs & GRUs

node.label: LSTMs & GRUs
node.definition: LSTMs and GRUs use learned gates to regulate writing, forgetting, and exposing recurrent state, reducing the vanishing-gradient limitations of basic RNNs.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, LSTMs & GRUs is modeled as depending on Recurrent neural networks, Gating & multiplicative control and feeding downstream nodes such as xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids, Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection.
examples: atlas downstream link to xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids
atlas downstream link to Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Recurrent neural networks, Gating & multiplicative control.

dependencies.rationale: LSTMs & GRUs extends or specializes the architecture family Recurrent neural networks.
LSTMs & GRUs is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.
In this atlas graph, LSTMs & GRUs has a `composes` dependency on Differentiable memory read/write with `common` strength.

sentence_units.text: LSTMs and GRUs use learned gates to regulate writing, forgetting, and exposing recurrent state, reducing the vanishing-gradient limitations of basic RNNs.
